name: Desfiladero de Bustelín (PR-AS 27) — circular desde Riodeporcos
region_intro: Hiking around Embalse de Salime offers diverse terrain in the western part of Asturias, Spain. The region is characterized by the large reservoir, formed by a 128-meter-high dam across the Navia River, surrounded by sierras, mountains, and fertile valleys. Hikers can expect routes that navigate through rugged landscapes dominated by shale and quartzite deposits, with views of the reservoir and its tributaries. The area also features historical elements, including the Camino Primitivo, and remnants of old villages that resurface when water levels are low. Best hiking trails around Embalse de Salime The most popular hiking route is Desfiladero de Bustelín (PR-AS 27) — circular desde Riodeporcos, a 9.7 miles (15.6 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 52 minutes to complete. This route explores a scenic gorge, offering a challenging experience. Another top favourite among local hikers is Monte Busbeirón – circular from Santalla, a difficult 12.3 miles (19.7 km) path. This longer route features significant elevation gain, providing expansive views of the surrounding mountainous terrain. Local hikers also love the Route of the environments of Grandas - circular from Grandas de Salime, a 9.6 miles (15.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes near the town, often completed in about 4 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Embalse de Salime is defined by reservoir views, mountainous terrain, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Embalse de Salime are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 90 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de Salime's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Embalse de Salime?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails around Embalse de Salime, offering a variety of experiences. These range from moderate paths to more challenging ascents, with options for different ability levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Embalse de Salime?
A: The terrain around Embalse de Salime is diverse, characterized by sierras, mountains, and fertile valleys. You'll encounter rugged landscapes dominated by shale and quartzite deposits, with routes navigating through forests and along the winding Navia River. Many trails offer magnificent views of the reservoir itself.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, among the available routes, there are 2 trails classified as easy. While specific easy routes are not detailed in the guide, the area does offer options for those seeking less strenuous walks.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Embalse de Salime area?
A: Absolutely. Many popular routes around Embalse de Salime are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240938491/desfiladero-de-bustelin-pr-as-27-circular-desde-riodeporcos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Bustelín (PR-AS 27) — circular desde Riodeporcos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047545115/route-of-the-environments-of-grandas-circular-from-grandas-de-salime" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the environments of Grandas - circular from Grandas de Salime&lt;/a&gt; are both excellent circular options.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints to look out for during my hike?
A: The Embalse de Salime area is rich in notable features. You can enjoy breathtaking panoramic vistas from various viewpoints, such as the 'Mirador del Embalse de Salime' on the AS-14 road, or the unique 'Mirador Boca de la Ballena,' a hanging viewpoint near the dam. The dam itself is a monumental feat of engineering, and when water levels are low, remnants of the old submerged village of Salime can occasionally be seen.

Q: Is Embalse de Salime a good destination for family hikes?
A: While the guide highlights more challenging routes, the region's diverse landscape and the presence of easier trails suggest that families can find suitable options. Look for routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances to ensure an enjoyable experience for all ages.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Embalse de Salime?
A: The region around Embalse de Salime is generally welcoming to dogs on trails, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Always practice responsible pet ownership by cleaning up after your dog.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Embalse de Salime?
A: The best time for hiking in Embalse de Salime is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, but be prepared for warmer temperatures, especially at midday. Winter hiking is possible but may require more preparation due to colder conditions and potential for rain.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting the hiking trails?
A: Parking is generally available near the starting points of many trails and in nearby villages like Grandas de Salime or Riodeporcos. Specific parking information for individual trailheads is often provided on komoot tour pages or local signage.

Q: Is it possible to reach the trailheads using public transport?
A: While the region is somewhat rural, the nearby town of Grandas de Salime serves as a hub. Public transport options might be limited directly to all trailheads, so checking local bus schedules to Grandas de Salime and planning for potential taxi services or carpooling for the final leg to more remote starting points is recommended.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails around Embalse de Salime?
A: The trails around Embalse de Salime are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 6 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning reservoir views, the challenging yet rewarding mountainous terrain, and the immersive experience of exploring the river valleys.

Q: Are there any nearby towns or attractions worth visiting after a hike?
A: Yes, the nearby town of Grandas de Salime is a cultural hub, home to the Ethnographic Museum 'Pepe el Ferreiro' and the collegiate church of El Salvador. You can learn more about the town's history and attractions on &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grandas_de_Salime" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikipedia&lt;/a&gt;. The dam itself also features a remarkable 60-meter mural by Joaquín Vaquero and his son, depicting its construction.

Q: What are some longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979825342/monte-busbeiron-circular-from-santalla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Busbeirón – circular from Santalla&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.3-mile (19.7 km) path with significant elevation gain, offering expansive views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240938491/desfiladero-de-bustelin-pr-as-27-circular-desde-riodeporcos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Bustelín (PR-AS 27) — circular desde Riodeporcos&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.7-mile (15.6 km) difficult trail exploring a scenic gorge.
tips: Suspension bridge that gives access to the village of Riodeporcos. Below we see the river Navia.
Magnificent views of the Navia River, which forms the border between Galicia and Asturias.
Small wooden bridge that allows us to cross the stream. Be careful that it does not have any fence.
A great path that allows us to enjoy the green and wild environment.
